title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet service file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kubelet service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the following command to list the user and group for the kubelet.service file on each node: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service done Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.default_value: By default, OpenShift sets the default user and group for the kubelet.service file to root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the cluster nodes**: Use the OpenShift CLI (oc) to access the list of nodes in your cluster. Run the command:
   ```
   oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'
   ```
2. **Debug each node**: For each node obtained in the previous step, run the following command to debug and access the kubelet service file:
   ```
   oc debug node/<node_name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service
   ```
   Replace `<node_name>` with the name of the node from the previous command.
3. **Verify ownership**: Check the output of the command for ownership details. 
4. **Expected secure configuration**: Ensure the ownership is listed as `root:root`.
5. **Confirm compliance**: If the ownership of the kubelet service file is `root:root` for all nodes, the control is compliant. If not, note the nodes that do not comply.
